one_liner: Early-warning system for supply chain planners

vision.problem_statement: Supply chain planners at mid-size manufacturers spend 8-10 hours a week manually reconciling demand forecasts across disconnected spreadsheets and ERP exports, discovering stockout risk only after it has already affected a customer order. There is no single place that surfaces "what's about to go wrong" early enough to act on it.
target_users: Demand planners at manufacturers with 3-10 distribution centers
Supply chain ops managers who own the weekly S&OP review
vision.vision_statement: PulseBoard becomes the early-warning system every planner checks before their first coffee - surfacing the three supply risks that matter most today, in plain language, before they become customer-facing problems.
vision.north_star_metric: Reduction in stockout-driven expedite shipments per quarter, measured per customer account.
non_goals: We are not building a full ERP replacement
We are not optimizing inbound freight routing in v1

research_questions: How do planners currently learn about stockout risk today?
What signals do they trust enough to act on immediately?
Where does the 8-10 hours a week of reconciliation actually go?
insights: Planners check 3-5 separate spreadsheets before every S&OP meeting, per shadowing sessions with 6 planners
Trust in automated alerts is low because past tools cried wolf on noisy SKUs
The real bottleneck is reconciling unit-of-measure mismatches between ERP and demand-planning exports

personas.name: Priya, Senior Demand Planner
personas.description: Owns forecast accuracy for 40 SKUs across 4 distribution centers, currently exports to Excel every Monday

personas.name: Marcus, Ops Manager
personas.description: Runs the weekly S&OP review, needs a one-page risk summary he can trust without re-checking the math

opportunity_areas: Automated reconciliation across ERP and demand-planning exports
Trustworthy, low-noise risk surfacing tied to real customer impact
A single pre-meeting summary view for the weekly S&OP review
